title: Partners

University of Twente
The University of Twente is a leading technical university with a strong focus on applied research, data science, and responsible AI.
…

title: UTKa DataLab
text: UTKa DataLab is a joint research initiative between Kadaster and the University of Twente, based in Apeldoorn. We develop methods to extract, combine, and analyze information from distributed data sources, with a strong commitment to ethical conduct, scientific integrity, and open science.

title: UTKa DataLab
text:
Research Themes

AI with Authoritative Data — grounding LLMs and AI systems in authoritative knowledge graphs
Trusted Federative Data Infrastructures — secure, privacy-respecting architectures for cross-system data sharing

title: LLM Enhancement via Knowledge Graphs
text: Grounding language models in the Kadaster Knowledge Graph to improve reasoning, reliability, and interpretability for land administration use cases.

title: Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Legacy Systems
text: Designing infrastructure for cross-system querying of heterogeneous data sources without physical data relocation, evaluated for performance and scalability at Kadaster.

title: Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Legacy Systems
Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Legacy Systems
Virtual Knowledge Graphs for Legacy Systems

text: Building a conversational interface that combines semantic knowledge graphs with LLM reasoning for intelligent retrieval of land administration data.

title: Security and Privacy-Aware Virtual Knowledge Graphs
text: Modeling privacy policies within graph ontologies and enforcing them technically, including when integrating large language models.

Research
text: UTKa DataLab’s research is organized around two themes, together spanning five active projects across PhD and EngD tracks.

title: Research
text:
AI with Authoritative Data
We investigate how Large Language Models and AI systems can be made more reliable and trustworthy when grounded in authoritative knowledge graphs. This addresses core challenges in government data applications, including hallucinations and limited explainability in AI-generated outputs. A key example of such a government knowledge graph is the Kadaster Knowledge Graph (Ronzhin et al. 2019).

AI Extraction from Notarial Deeds

Transparent AI system design for automated information extraction from notarial deeds.

title: Research
text:
Trusted Federative Data Infrastructures
We design secure data-sharing architectures that respect privacy and data sovereignty. Our work centers on Virtual Knowledge Graph (VKG) infrastructures that enable efficient cross-system querying without requiring data replication or centralization.
